etymology.description: 艹(艹) (cǎo, "grass") + 樂(樂) (lè, "music/joy") = plant-based substance associated with well-being -> medicine
etymology.interpretation: The character 藥(yào, "medicine") combines the semantic component 艹(艹) (cǎo, "grass"), indicating its herbal nature, with the phonetic component 樂(樂) (lè, "joy/music"), suggesting a beneficial effect, to convey the idea of medicine or a healing substance.
etymology.simplification: 藥(藥) was simplified to 药(藥) by replacing the phonetic component 樂(樂) with the simpler 丶(丶) element, significantly reducing the number of strokes.

examples.chinese: 我需要一些感冒药(我需要一些感冒藥)。
医生给我开了止痛药(醫生給我開了止痛藥)。
examples.pinyin: Yīshēng gěi wǒ kāile zhǐtòng yào.

examples.chinese: 这种药可以帮助你睡眠(這種藥可以幫助你睡眠)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè zhǒng yào kěyǐ bāngzhù nǐ shuìmián.
examples.english: This medicine can help you sleep.
